title: Tetracera delima
taxonomy: Tetracera delima is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae. It belongs to the genus Tetracera, which comprises several species of plants native to tropical regions. The Dilleniaceae family is an ancient group of flowering plants that are thought to have originated over 100 million years ago. Tetracera delima is a member of the order Dilleniales, which is a small but distinct group of flowering plants. This classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary relationships between different plant species.
uses: Tetracera delima is known for its various medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, particularly in India, to treat various health conditions. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Tetracera delima is also used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal uses and benefits.
